title: Goniopteris vivipara
taxonomy: Goniopteris vivipara is a species of fern, specifically belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. Within the Thelypteridaceae family, Goniopteris vivipara is classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the Polypodiales order, it shares characteristics with other ferns in this group, such as having compound leaves and a vascular system. This species is further classified under the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a subcategory of the class Polypodiopsida. The classification of Goniopteris vivipara is still subject to ongoing research and updates in the field of botany.
uses: Goniopteris vivipara is a species of fern that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and infections. In some parts of the world, the plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
